message:
refactor(claudeconfig): track settings_file_existed; tidy; more coverage

Review-pass fixes on top of the initial #110 landing:

1. **Track SettingsFileExisted in the snapshot.**

   Snapshot now records whether settings.json existed at all pre-swarm,
   in addition to whether it had a `model` field. That distinguishes
   three cases:

     - SettingsFileExisted=false, HadModelField=false  — no file at all
     - SettingsFileExisted=true,  HadModelField=false  — file without `model`
     - SettingsFileExisted=true,  HadModelField=true   — file with `model`

   Restore uses the new flag to correctly restore the "no file at all"
   case: it deletes the settings.json after clearing the model field if
   (a) the user had no file pre-swarm AND (b) the only thing in the
   settings.json post-clear is an empty `{}`. If the swarm (or Claude
   Code) added other fields — theme, mcp_servers, etc. — we leave the
   file alone since we don't know whether those represent intentional
   user state or swarm byproducts.

   Previously, Restore in this scenario left a stub `{}` settings.json
   the user didn't have before — technically equivalent to "no
   settings" for Claude Code, but a visible filesystem artifact the
   user would be right to wonder about.

2. **Drop the custom trimSpace helper.**

   strings.TrimSpace is stdlib and adding the strings import is a
   non-cost in a package that already imports encoding/json. The
   custom helper was a premature "stdlib-minimal" flinch that didn't
   buy anything.

3. **Fix the #nosec comments.**

   The previous "# comes from ResolveClaudeSettingsPath" comments
   were misleading — these are library functions that accept
   caller-supplied paths. Replaced with explicit "caller-supplied
   path (library function)" annotations that accurately describe
   the trust boundary.

4. **Five new tests covering the new behavior.**

   - TestSnapshotRestoreWhenNoSettingsFileExistedPreSwarm — round-trip
     where settings.json didn't exist pre-swarm and shouldn't exist
     post-restore.
   - TestRestoreKeepsSettingsWithOtherFieldsWhenUserHadNoFile — the
     key non-destruction guarantee: if the settings.json the swarm
     materialized has more than just `model`, we preserve the other
     fields.
   - TestRemoveIfEmptyObjectNoOpsOnNonEmpty / RemovesEmpty /
     NoOpsOnMissing / NoOpsOnNonJSON — full coverage of the
     conservative cleanup helper.

Total: 20 tests pass (14 from the initial commit + 6 new here), go
vet clean, gofmt clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
